title: [SUPPORT]  Is there any hope for future support and development of CAD?
author: Bamajon1974
body:
### Acknowledgements

- [x] I acknowledge that I have read the above items

### Please describe what you need support with

Is there any hope for future support and development of CAD? Questions and requests have remained unanswered for months. It would be nice if the developer would acknowledge. Are there any viable forks that have been updated and under active development? Or replacement cookie managers altogether? 
Thanks!

title: [FEATURE REQUEST]  better cleaning delay
author: e00E
body:
### Acknowledgements

- [x] I acknowledge that I have read the above items

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

The [current](https://github.com/Cookie-AutoDelete/Cookie-AutoDelete/wiki/Documentation#delay-before-automatic-cleaning) delay setting works like this:

Tab closes. Wait X seconds. Clean everything. 

This is not the behavior I expect from a delay setting. It's confusing. I can set a delay of 10 minutes, close a tab, come back after 5 minutes and the cookies are gone because cleanup was triggered from something else.

### Describe the solution that you'd like

Instead I expect it to work like this:

Tab closes. Wait X seconds. Clean cookies from the domain of the tab.

I want a delay *per site*. So that within the delay, I can reopen the site and still have my cookies. Only after the last tab of the site has been closed for X time, then the cookies should be deleted.


### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: [FEATURE REQUEST]  Documentation on cleaning more data types
author: Joebugg
body:
### Acknowledgements

- [x] I acknowledge that I have read the above items

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

https://github.com/Cookie-AutoDelete/Cookie-AutoDelete/wiki/Documentation  "Upon enabling any of the following site data cleanup options, ALL existing data for that type will be cleared."
Needs clarity.

### Describe the solution that you'd like

This should warn people if it will indeed delete said data even for whitelisted domains, or clarify that it's safe and only goes after the unlisted and grey domains.  A large number of users are likely to assume it does when it doesn't, or doesn't when it does.  It's ambiguous.


### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional Context

The closest issue I saw open, is #467, dealing with onboarding of new users.

title: [FEATURE REQUEST]  improve notification text
author: gcb
body: ### Acknowledgements - [x] I acknowledge that I have read the above items ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. The current notification makes it very difficult to gather any information on most systems as it is too verbose to offer any meaningful information. e.g. All that can be seen is the text template mostly. Most of the information is either mashed in the text (6 cookies) or barely fits (a single domain made it before the cut, often not even that!) I suggest a more concise wording so that at least domains and cookie count can be glanced from the popup right away. ### Describe the solution that you'd like - remove the title and version. there's already the icon. also, it can be at the *end* of the message if the user is curious and expand the full text. - title should be just the verb and count. e.g. "Deleted 6 cookies (1 domain)" - description should be the list of domains - sorted? alphabetically? by recency? by amount of data? - details (site-data also deleted, extension info, etc) at the very end. ### Describe alternatives you've considered looked for options that don't require code change. couldn't find anything. ### Additional Context _No response_

title: [Bug] Data for websites is not deleted

body:

title: [Bug] meet.google.com Cookie/Site data is retained when Manually or automatically cleaning data
author: muzzah
body:
### Acknowledgements

- [x] I acknowledge that I have read the above items

### Describe the bug

I noticed that even though I have automatic cleaning of all data, meet.google.com seems to remember my account. I am using Ungoogled Chromium with a number of extensions installed including Cookie auto delete.

### To Reproduce

1. Have auto delete turned on in CAD and enable everything to be deleted. 
2. Log into your google account at meet.google.com
3. Close tab
4. Wait a while
5. Go back to meet.google.com and you will see that it still remembers your account. 
6. Manually deleting has not effect either

### Expected Behavior

meet.google.com should not remember logged in account. 

### Screenshots

Havent added due to ease of replication

### System Info - Operating System (OS)

MacOS 15.5

### System Info - Browser Info

Ungoogled Chromium Version 137.0.7151.55 (Official Build, ungoogled-chromium) (arm64)

### System Info - CookieAutoDelete Version

3.8.2

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: [Bug] Exported Expression JSON between sync'ed containers is imported wrong.
author: OgalFinklestein
body:
### Acknowledgements

- [x] I acknowledge that I have read the above items

### Describe the bug

I have a 20 containers in FireFox that are sync'ed up between 3 browsers.
I can export Expressions from the Win10 profile and re-import them back into the same profile, no problem.
If I take that same JSON file and import it into my Linux profile, only the rules for the four default containers (Personal, Work, Banking, Shopping) import correctly.  The rules for the self-made containers are imported correctly.

Looking at the exported JSON file, the containers are referenced with generic names like `firefox-container-24`, which is fine for the source profile that exported it, but importing it over -- those container IDs dont line up.  For example, in the Win10 profile the `games` container is `firefox-container-24`, but in Linux it's `firefox-container-11`

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/76843e6f-8451-4088-8f9b-6443355905ba)

So it associates the rules to the wrong container.  Even in a newly created profile that is sync'ed up the number is neither `24` nor `11`.  The only consistent container numbers are `1`-`4`:

* firefox-container-1 ( Personal )
* firefox-container-2 ( Work )
* firefox-container-3 ( Banking )
* firefox-container-4 ( Shopping )




### To Reproduce

* Create a new Firefox profile.
* Install "Firefox Multi-Account Containers" (FMAC).
* Sign-in to Firefox Sync to sync browser profile.
  * Make sure FMAC is also sync'ed (not by default).
* Create some additional containers.
* Install Cookie-AutoDelete.
  * create some lists for each container.
  * export Expressions.
* Sync up profile and containers to cloud.
  * *optionally* close out your browser.

---

* Create another new Firefox profile.
* Install FMAC.
* Sign-in to Firefox Sync to sync browser profile.
  * Make sure FMAC containers are pulled down from the cloud.
* Install Cookie-AutoDelete.
  * import Expressions.

See if your rules import correctly or not.




### Expected Behavior

Well, since CAD obviously knows the name of the container itself (as shown in the screenshot), it would seem better to save the rules by the container's name and not some ID.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### System Info - Operating System (OS)

Windows and Linux

### System Info - Browser Info

standard Firefox (Windows) and Firefox ESR (Linux)

### System Info - CookieAutoDelete Version

3.8.2

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: [SUPPORT]  What's the difference between the two domains shown?
author: MenonPlayz
body:
### Acknowledgements

- [x] I acknowledge that I have read the above items

### Please describe what you need support with

I haven't found any explaination in these two options. The UI doesnt explain this, neither do the Documentation (Only explains settings) or the FAQ (Only questions regarding the other buttons in the Popup).
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1314f561-532d-4005-86d5-b14eff0be8cf)

I know the * stands for wildcard, so Im guessing Ill whitelist any subdomain of the base domain but not the base domain itself? Or is it the other way around? Honestly confusing and I dont get why there should be a difference. 

Sorry if this is a duplicate of some other issue, couldn't find one under the terms that I was searching for.

title: [Bug]  Cloudflare challenges impossible to pass with CAD enabled
author: EidolonEntropy
body:
### Acknowledgements

- [x] I acknowledge that I have read the above items

### Describe the bug

Cloudflare turnsile challenges, the ones where they take over the whole page you're viewing with a gray screen and say '[site] needs to review the security of your connection before proceeding', are unsolvable with CAD enabled. I've been having this issue for... years? I think years. I finally got to the bottom of it being caused by this addon. I tried whitelisting challenges.cloudflare.com, no dice. I can't find a more specific domain or anything else to the effect. Humbly requesting either the proper string to let Cloudflare through or a feature toggle for 'enable Cloudflare challenges' which does the same thing in-app.

### To Reproduce

For me I can surefire trigger it by visiting [CCGCastle](https://ccgcastle.com) and attempting to log in. Attempt to solve the challenge, it will spin, refresh, and repeat ad infinitum.

### Expected Behavior

Challenge resolves immediately. Cookies + other data are not removed for Cloudflare challenges.

### Screenshots

This is the only relevant key I can find in Storage.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/438cd4b2-ea76-42e2-9392-a5aba3ad9b79)

### System Info - Operating System (OS)

Windows 11 

### System Info - Browser Info

Firefox Nightly

### System Info - CookieAutoDelete Version

3.8.2

### Additional Context

Disabling the addon makes Cloudflare behave properly, and other container tabs also make it behave properly in certain cases where the site wasn't previously accessed in that container. 
Also: while CCGCastle isn't whitelisted for me, I have another site that causes the same issue whitelisted and it has no effect on the challenge.

title: [FEATURE REQUEST]  Iterate through `localStorage` with `for..in` or `Object.getOwnPropertyNames` to get all keys (like cookies), then allow the user to select which keys to keep

body:

- [x] I acknowledge that I have read the above items

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

`localStorage` should match the cookie implementation. Not just invoke `Storage.prototype.clear`.

### Describe the solution that you'd like

Title.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I use uBO to set specific `localStorage` and `cookie` keys, but sometimes you want to exclude some, and keep all the rest.

### Additional Context
